rules.text: A person shall not be admitted as a student for any degree in the Faculty of Commerce unless the person has fulfilled all admission criteria as approved by Council.
The right of admission to lectures, classes and seminars is restricted to students who are specifically registered for the courses concerned.
Students must complete all requirements for the degree within the standard number of years.
Candidates may not sit the final examination if they have not been granted a Duly Performed Certificate (DP).
Students who fail a University course twice will not be allowed to register for the same course a third time.
Should a required course be failed twice (including DPR, INC, AB, UF, SM or SF), the student has to transfer to a programme of study where the same course is not required.
A student may not repeat a course already passed.
A student shall not be admitted to a course for which pre-requisites have not been obtained.
A student may not register for a combination of courses with a timetable clash.
A student may, in special circumstances, apply to the Dean for a concession to deviate from a programme curriculum prescribed in the schedule.
A student may not take more than the total number of courses stipulated for the year of the specialisation.
A student may change their qualification and/or programme of study with the permission of the Dean. Not all courses passed in the previous programme may be credited towards a new qualification.
Exemption from the requirements of the degree may be granted to students who have completed courses at this or other approved universities.

Senate may permit a candidate to write a supplementary examination in one or more courses failed. A student will be granted a supplementary examination where only one outstanding course remains for the completion of the degree, and the student attained a final mark between 40% and 44%.
The degree may be awarded: with distinction, with distinction in one or more subjects, or with distinction in the degree and with distinction in one or more subjects.
Students studying Law by Commerce programs may only proceed to the academic year including the PVL 1000-level courses if they have received confirmation of a place on those courses.
Must obtain an aggregate of at least 63% for all courses in the prescribed curriculum prior to the year of including the PVL courses.

Students who fail any one of MAM1031F/MAM1032S/MAM1005H/MAM1006H/STA1006S/STA1106H/ECO1010F/ECO1110F/ECO1011S/ECO1111S/ACC1006F/ACC1106F/ACC1011S/ACC1111S will be required to leave all Actuarial Science degrees.
A student will be required to complete all compulsory and optional courses prescribed for each year of study to proceed to courses prescribed for the following year.
A student who fails no more than four semester courses in any year may be permitted to proceed to the next year of study.
All the degree rules for undergraduate degrees (FBA) apply unless specified below.
The curriculum shall consist of a minimum of 528 NQF credits of which 120 NQF credits must be at NQF level 8.
A student shall not be permitted to renew registration if they fail any course required for the degree more than once.
Must complete: 4 semester courses by end of year 1; 10 by end of year 2; 18 by end of year 3.
Must not fail the equivalent of seven or more semester courses during the period of registration.
Must complete minimum programme requirements per specialisation as specified in the handbook.
All the degree rules for undergraduate degrees (FBA) apply unless specified below.
The curriculum shall consist of a minimum of 440 NQF credits of which 120 NQF credits must be at NQF Level 7.
A student shall not be permitted to renew registration if they fail any course required for the degree more than once.
Must complete: 4 semester courses by end of year 1; 8 by end of year 2 (including specialisation courses); 15 by end of year 3.
Must not fail the equivalent of seven or more semester courses during the period of registration.

Students not achieving DP status in ACC1006F by end of first year of study; or not passing all required first year courses and obtaining 60% for ACC1011S by end of second year; or not passing ACC2012W by end of third year will not be permitted to renew registration.

rule_sets.applies_to: Bachelor of Commerce in Actuarial Science

rules.text: All the degree rules for undergraduate degrees (FBA) and the Bachelor of Business Science (FBB) apply.
The curriculum shall consist of a minimum of 528 NQF credits of which 120 will be at NQF Level 7.

Students are allowed to register for BU2016H if they have achieved at least 60% average for ACC1006F & ACC1011S, and ECO1010F/S & ECO1011F (OR for the EDU-equivalents), AND one of four conditions involving MAM/STA averages.

bcom.requirements: No failures. Completed in standard number of years. Courses passed at supplementary exam do not count as failures.
bcom.weighting: Course levels 1 and 2 weighted 1, course level 3 weighted 2. Only first attempt and only courses required for the programme.

bbusc.requirements: No failures. Completed in standard time.
bbusc.weighting: Course levels 1 and 2 weighted 1, course levels 3 and 4 weighted 2. Only first attempt and only courses required for the programme.

course_distinctions.actuarial_science: 75% or above for each of Actuarial Science II Models, Contingencies, and Actuarial Science III courses
course_distinctions.computer_science: Average 75% or above for CSC 2001/2002, and average 75% for CSC 3002/3003
course_distinctions.economics_bcom: Average 80% across ECO3020F and two other 3000-level ECO courses, with at least 75% in at least two
course_distinctions.economics_bbusc: Same as BCom PLUS average 75% for ECO4000-level courses at first attempt, with subminimum 70% for coursework and research paper
course_distinctions.finance_bbusc: Average mark for FTX3044F and FTX3045S of 75% or more; average for FTX4056S, FTX4057F and FTX4087S of 75% or more; with a subminimum of 70% for each of five courses
course_distinctions.finance_bcom: Average 75% or more for FTX2024F/S and average 75% for FTX3044F and FTX3045S combined
course_distinctions.information_systems: 75% weighted average for all final year Information Systems courses
course_distinctions.marketing: Weighted average of 75% for BUS4026W, BUS4052H, BUS4058F, BUS3041F, BUS3043S and BUS3008W, with subminimum of 70% for each
course_distinctions.statistics: 75% in two 2000-level and two 3000-level courses required for the major subject
course_distinctions.management_accounting: 80% or above for Management Accounting I and 75% or above for Management Accounting II
course_distinctions.taxation: 80% or above for Taxation I and 75% or above for Taxation II
course_distinctions.mathematics: At least 75% for Mathematics II and Mathematics III
course_distinctions.io_psychology: At least 75% overall for BUS4006W and BUS4030H, obtaining not less than 70% for both the coursework and the research report components
